title: Qatari Sand Cats Caught on Camera for the First Time
gist: A three-year study in Frontiers in Conservation Science documented sand cats on camera in Qatar for the first time, confirming the elusive, declining desert felines — thought to have vanished from the country — still hold a small foothold.

insights.claim: Sand cats, believed to have vanished completely from Qatar, were documented on camera there for the first time in a three-year study published in Frontiers in Conservation Science, confirming a surviving population.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Camera-trap conservation surveys keep recovering presumed-lost species; basic ecology with no market read.
insights.quote: the species has maintained a furry foothold in a small corner of the country
insights.locator: According to a three-year study recently published

insights.claim: Sand cats are adapted to extreme desert heat — fur-soled paws to resist hot sand, camouflaging khaki coats, and nocturnal burrow-dwelling to escape temperatures above 120°F.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.quote: The soles of their paws are carpeted with long hair to protect them from the hot sand
insights.locator: The sand cat’s feet are actually furry
